title: podocarpus coriaceus
intro: Podocarpus coriaceus, also known as the Yellow Podocarp, is a slow-growing, evergreen tree or shrub native to New Zealand, belonging to the Podocarpaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The health benefits of Podocarpus coriaceus include reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and promoting wound healing, making it a valuable herbal remedy. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to support overall well-being and alleviate symptoms of certain health conditions.
